title: Orchis × dolicheilos
intro: Orchis × dolicheilos, also known as the hybrid bee orchid, is a rare and unique plant species resulting from the cross between the orchid species Orchis mascula and Ophrys sphegodes. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Orchis × dolicheilos include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments to support wound healing and skin health.
taxonomy: Orchis × dolicheilos is a hybrid orchid resulting from the cross between Orchis mascula and Orchidala diantha, but more specifically a hybrid between Orchis mascula and Orchis italica. The classification of Orchis × dolicheilos can be complex due to the involvement of multiple species. This species is part of the Orchidaceae family and the genus Orchis. As a hybrid, Orchis × dolicheilos is not a distinct species but rather a cross between two parent species. The classification of Orchis × dolicheilos is primarily based on its parentage rather than its own unique characteristics.
uses: Orchis × dolicheilos is known as a hybrid orchid, but it doesn't have much information on its medicinal uses. However, its parent species, Orchis mascula, has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Orchis mascula's roots are said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It has been used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Additionally, its flowers have been used in herbal teas to aid in digestion.
properties: Orchis × dolicheilos has been used in traditional medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help in the treatment of various health conditions. The plant is also thought to possess antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making it a potential natural remedy for infections and other diseases. However, more research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic benefits of Orchis × dolicheilos. Further studies are necessary to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.

preparations: Orchis × dolicheilos is a hybrid orchid, and information on its herbal preparations is limited.
